title: Combining moment matrices, symmetric extension, and Lovász theta: $Φ_{\text{E8}}$ is entangled
authors: Jȩdrzej Stempin, Gerard Anglès Munné, Santiago Llorens, Felix Huber
abstract: We solve an open problem in entanglement theory posed by Yu et al., {\it Nature Communications 12, 1012 (2021)}. The problem is to show, via an entanglement witness, that the $14$-qubit state $Φ_{\text{E8}}$ is entangled. Inspired by a method from quantum codes, we combine symmetric extension with moment matrices to prove that $Φ_{\text{E8}}$ is entangled. The proof has the form of a rational infeasibility certificate for a semidefinite program, yielding an explicit entanglement witness. Our approach unifies and extends several earlier methods that involve the Lovász theta number of the Pauli anti-commutativity graph, promising scalability and flexibility in further applications.
summary: This paper solves a long-standing open problem by proving that the 14-qubit state Phi_E8 is entangled. By combining symmetric extensions with moment matrices and graph theory, the authors provide an explicit, mathematically rigorous entanglement witness. This method offers a scalable framework for detecting entanglement in complex multipartite systems.
main_problem: The paper aims to solve an open problem by providing an explicit entanglement witness to prove that the 14-qubit state Phi_E8 is entangled.
main_result: The authors successfully prove the entanglement of the Phi_E8 state by providing a rational infeasibility certificate for a semidefinite program, effectively constructing an operational entanglement witness.
method: The approach combines the symmetric extension hierarchy with the non-commutative optimization hierarchy (moment matrices) and utilizes symmetry reduction via the Terwilliger algebra to make the semidefinite program (SDP) computationally tractable.
model_or_system: The system is a 14-qubit multipartite state, Phi_E8, which is constructed from projectors onto symmetric and antisymmetric subspaces of qubit pairs and is related to the existence of AME(7,2) states.
key_observables: The entanglement witness W_Phi_E8, derived from the Lovasz theta number of the Pauli anti-commutativity graph.
important_parameters: The weight of Pauli strings, the anti-commutativity graph G_4+, and the weight threshold delta = 4.
main_assumptions: The proof relies on a relaxation of the SDP via group averaging, though the authors note that infeasibility of the relaxed version implies infeasibility of the original.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a visual summary of the proof sketch, showing the transition from a putative separable state to a constrained three-copy extension.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of Phi_E8 entanglement, describes the construction of the symmetric extension and moment matrix, details the symmetry reduction and SDP formulation, presents the numerical infeasibility certificate, and concludes with the construction of the explicit entanglement witness.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. The putative separable state ΦE8 allows for a sym- metric three-copy extension Φ(3) E8 , resulting in a highly con- strained moment matrix that is nonlinear in expectations. Via semidefinite programming (SDP) duality we show that no such moment matrix exists, proving entanglement in ΦE8.
